items.title: Form One student critical after fall from school building in Ipoh
items.summary: Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah has ordered a full report into the incident involving the student. "My visit was also accompanied by Education Ministry director-general Datuk Dr Azman Adnan," he said in a Facebook post today. Khairudin said he had instructed the Kinta District Education Office and the Perak Education Department to prepare a comprehensive report on the incident.

items.title: Abdul Aziz Hassan remembered for integrity, strong newsroom standards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former New Straits Times journalist Abdul Aziz Hassan is remembered as a principled and exacting newsroom figure who left a lasting mark on Malaysian journalism. Former journalist Zaidi Azmi remembered Abdul Aziz as a loud, opinionated and headstrong editor who played a significant role in shaping younger reporters. Former New Straits Times journalist Abdul Aziz Hassan died this morning.

items.title: Pick-up, jeep owners can apply for extra 100 litres under Budi Diesel
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Owners of diesel pick-up trucks and jeeps can apply for an additional 100 litres of subsidised fuel per month, bringing their total monthly quota to 300 litres under the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) mechanism. For other eligible private diesel vehicle owners, a base limit of 200 litres per month has been set. It also gives recipients flexibility to manage their fuel use according to their monthly needs," he said at a press conference at the Finance Ministry today.

items.title: Singaporean driver escapes unhurt as MPV catches fire on PLUS highway
items.summary: MUAR: A Singaporean escaped unhurt after the multi-purpose vehicle he was driving was almost completely destroyed in a blaze at Km100.1 of the North-South Expressway (southbound) here this morning. Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Husaini Eddy Mustafa said the man's Renault Grand Scenic was found to be 95 per cent destroyed upon firefighters' arrival. "He managed to exit the vehicle safely and was not injured in the incident," Husaini said in a statement today.

items.title: PN Supreme Council holds emergency meeting tonight
items.summary: PN chairman and Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin arrived at the party's headquarters at 8.28pm, accompanied by Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu, Women's chief Datuk Nolee Ashilin Mohammed Radzi and Armada chief Muhammad Hilman Idham. According to a notice dated June 18 and signed by PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, the meeting was convened under Clause 10 (Notice of Meetings and Agenda) and Clause 12 (Frequency of Meetings) of the coalition's constitution. Takiyuddin had previously said that any activity or meeting using the PN name could not be carried out without the approval of PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Monday, June 22, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1470085]] [[nid:1470084]] [[nid:1470083]] [[nid:1470082]] [[nid:1470081]] [[nid:1470080]] [[nid:1470079]] [[nid:1470078]] [[nid:1470077]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 22, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Here are today's top stories: Populist politicians Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim has taken a swipe at populist politicians who prioritise social media theatrics over governance, with his remarks widely interpreted as a jab at Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman. Tougher penalties The Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026, which seeks to strengthen regulatory and enforcement powers under the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333), was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. Trial begins Two married couples linked to a viral video showing the alleged abuse of their domestic helpers claimed trial in separate magistrates' courts here today to charges of causing hurt, criminal intimidation and unlawful possession of passports.

items.title: Four-vehicle crash outside Istana Bukit Serene leaves three people injured
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Three people were injured in a multi-vehicle collision involving three cars and a motorcycle in front of the main gate of Istana Bukit Serene here this morning. The 8.20am accident involved three cars, including a Volkswagen Jetta, and a motorcycle. Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Abd Wahab Nasir said firefighters found a female victim trapped inside one of the cars, while the others managed to get out of their vehicles.

items.title: Theta Edge chairman steps down
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Theta Edge Bhd chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahman Mamat has resigned to pursue other commitments. Theta Edge said there were no disagreements between Abdul Rahman and the board. He is also associated with MCE Holdings Bhd and ECA Integrated Solution Bhd, according to the filing.

items.title: Nine foreigners suspected of online scam activities detained in Pengkalan Kubor
items.summary: Also detained was a 25-year-old Thai national believed to be a tekong darat responsible for transporting the migrants. Ahmad Radzi said checks on the vehicle uncovered nine migrants comprising six Chinese men, one Chinese woman, one Myanmar man and one Myanmar woman. [[nid:1470052]] Ahmad Radzi said officers also seized 22 mobile phones, a Dell laptop, a laptop bag, a Chinese passport and a Thai identity card.

items.title: ICT Zone secures RM28.1mil hardware leasing deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ICT Zone Asia Bhd has secured a RM28.13 million order for the leasing of information and communication technology (ICT) hardware through its wholly-owned ICT Zone Ventures Bhd. ICT Zone Ventures received the purchase order from MRA International Sdn Bhd on June 19 for the leasing of ICT hardware over 36 months. ICT Zone said it intends to utilise bank borrowings and internally generated funds to finance the purchase of ICT hardware for the lease.

items.title: KJ leaves Negri Sembilan candidacy decision to Umno leadership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Rembau member of parliament Khairy Jamaluddin has left it to the party leadership to decide on the possibility of him contesting in the Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1. It was previously reported that the Umno Supreme Council had recorded 6,252 applications from former members seeking to rejoin the party, as well as applications from new members. They include former foreign minister Tan Sri Syed amid Albar, former defence minister Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Tun Hussein , and Khairy, who is also a former Umno Youth chief.

items.title: Anti-graft watchdog wants more transparency in corruption compound cases
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) has welcomed the Attorney-General's Chambers' (A-GC) clarification on the use of compounds in corruption-linked cases but says greater transparency is still needed to maintain public confidence in the country's anti-corruption framework. However, he said concerns remain over whether sufficient information is provided to the public when high-profile corruption cases are resolved through compounds and when charges are subsequently withdrawn. "We do not dispute the legality of compounds under AMLATFPUAA or the discretion vested in the public prosecutor.

items.title: Kevin Warsh signals hawkish shift in first Fed press conference: Franklin Templeton CIO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kevin Warsh may be the most hawkish US Federal Reserve (Fed) chair since Paul Volcker in the 1980s, according to Franklin Templeton Fixed Income chief investment officer Sonal Desai. "First impressions count, and Kevin Warsh came out as a hawk in his first press conference as the new Fed chair. Desai said Warsh had also rejected the idea that the Fed must choose between inflation control and employment.

items.title: Canada's annual inflation rate surges to a 29-month high of 3.2pct in May
items.summary: Analysts polled by Reuters had estimated the annual inflation rate to touch 3 per cent in May, up from 2.8 per cent in April. The monthly inflation rate rose to 1 per cent in May, exceeding expectations of 0.8 per cent rise. Gasoline prices in May rose by 33.2 per cent on a year-over-year basis.

items.title: LFE Corp unit secures RM16.21mil contract from Puncak Concept
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: LFE Corporation Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, LFE Engineering Sdn Bhd, has secured a RM16.21 million contract from Puncak Concept Sdn Bhd for piling and related construction works for a development project in Cyberjaya, Selangor. The contract period covers seven months, commencing from June 20, 2026 to Jan 20, 2027. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Finance minister II: Too early to raise Budi95 quota back to 300 litres
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government is not yet considering restoring the Budi95 monthly fuel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres, saying it is still assessing the impact of recent developments in the Middle East. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the ceasefire announced recently was still in its early stages and it remained unclear whether it would hold long enough to ease global oil market uncertainties. Amir Hamzah was responding to a question on whether Putrajaya was considering reinstating the Budi95 quota to 300 litres a month following recent declines in fuel prices.

items.title: 42 Myanmar workers, two managers detained in Johor immigration raid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A total of 42 foreign workers and two local managers were detained in an Immigration Department operation targeting restaurants suspected of employing and harbouring undocumented migrants here on Friday (June 19). Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operation uncovered multiple immigration offences involving foreign workers believed to have breached the conditions of their passes or worked illegally. They are being investigated for alleged offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963.

items.title: #NSTviral: Hilux–Viva road rage incident draws public outrage
items.summary: The two-minute 43-second video, shared on the DashCam Malaysia Facebook page, purportedly shows a black Toyota Hilux using the emergency lane along the PLUS Expressway between Jawi and Bukit Tambun before abruptly cutting into the fast lane. The manoeuvre appeared to result in minor contact with a silver Perodua Viva travelling in the right lane. The situation escalated moments later when the Viva overtook several vehicles before returning to the fast lane directly in front of the Hilux.

items.title: Johor raises allocation to RM5mil for Muar police quarters restoration
items.summary: MUAR: The caretaker Johor government has increased funding for the upgrading and repair of the district police quarters here to RM5 million after inspections revealed more extensive damage than initially anticipated. Caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state had originally approved RM1 million for repair and maintenance works following his visit to the quarters in March, where he found residents grappling with longstanding structural and safety issues. "As a result, the state government approved an additional RM4 million, bringing the total allocation to RM5 million to rehabilitate four blocks of quarters," he said in a statement today after inspecting the upgrading works on Friday.

items.title: PEOPLElogy marks 25 years, launches national "I'm AI Ready" push
items.summary: The programme is anchored on the Malaysia AI Readiness Index 2026-2030 and aims to equip five million people with AI-related skills by 2028. It will focus on awareness, structured training, certification pathways and industry-linked programmes, aligned with national frameworks including the 13th Malaysia Plan and MyDigital. The initiative is structured around public awareness, skills assessment and education, and industry collaboration.

items.title: 16 arrested, 260 motorcycles seized in Johor illegal racing crackdown
items.summary: The operation involving the Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department, Johor police and the Road Transport Department (RTD) was conducted to curb persistent "road thugs" activities that endanger other road users. Iskandar Puteri district police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said 334 motorists and passengers were screened during the operation, while 251 motorcycles and 68 cars were inspected. Police issued 604 summonses for various traffic offences, while RTD issued another 127 summonses and notices involving 68 cars.

items.title: Demolition of Rohingya-linked Sungai Tekali structures nearly complete, says MP
items.summary: HULU LANGAT: Demolition works involving structures linked to the Rohingya community at Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Sungai Tekali, are nearing completion, with most of the illegal buildings already torn down. Hulu Langat member of parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan said an officer visited the site today to assess progress and verify reports that the operation was entering its final stage. Only a small section of the main illegally constructed building remains standing," he said.

items.title: RM2.10 diesel from July 1: Who qualifies, how it works and how much fuel you can buy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government will roll out a new MyKad-based diesel subsidy system from July 1, allowing eligible Malaysians to buy diesel at RM2.10 per litre while moving all diesel prices to market rates nationwide. The move marks the biggest change to the Budi Diesel programme since targeted diesel subsidies were introduced in June 2024, replacing monthly cash assistance for individual recipients with direct fuel subsidies at the pump. The government estimates around 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will qualify.

items.title: Widad Group executive deputy chairman resigns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Widad Group Bhd executive deputy chairman Tan Sri Muhammad Ikmal Opat Abdullah has resigned from the board effective today. According to the filing, Muhammad Ikmal held a direct interest of 1.77 million shares and an indirect interest of 424.64 million shares in the construction company. Widad shares closed at two sen today, valuing the company at RM62.42 million.

items.title: 700,000 to benefit from MyKad-based Budi Diesel subsidy from July 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: More than 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will gain access to targeted diesel subsidies from July 1, with eligibility verified through MyKad under the new Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) mechanism. Early access to the system will open on June 27 for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia before the full nationwide rollout begins. "This diesel subsidy targeting reform aims to streamline the diesel subsidy mechanism nationwide through a single system that is uniform, transparent and easy to use.

items.title: Propel Global expands into property development with RM64mil GDV Riverpoint project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Propel Global Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Propel Global Development Sdn Bhd, is developing a project in Kuantan, Pahang -- Riverpoint, among the city's first premium riverfront commercial shoplot development with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM64 million. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the oil and gas (O&G) services provider said Riverpoint marked Propel Global Development's maiden commercial property development project. "Riverpoint marks an important milestone in Propel Global's strategic diversification journey and our expansion into property development.

items.title: From sidelines to centre stage, Andy Burnham on course to be UK's next prime minister
items.summary: MANCHESTER: Four years ago Andy Burnham complained he was repeatedly denied a main-stage speaking slot at the Labour Party conference under Prime Minister Keir Starmer's leadership, saying it was a sign of disrespect for his then position as Greater Manchester mayor. Burnham "has proven his credentials in putting forward a Labour vision voters can rally around", said Alex Sobel, a Labour member of Parliament. On Monday, Burnham said he would stand to be Labour leader, while one of his main rivals, former health minister Wes Streeting, said he would not oppose him in a leadership contest.

items.title: Johor Regent grants an audience with PM Anwar, meeting reaffirms close ties between Putrajaya and state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, granted an audience to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Tunku here earlier today. He reaffirmed that the federal government will continue to maintain good relations and close cooperation with its Johor counterpart. “Tunku Mahkota Ismail and I also agreed to continue to maintain good relations, in addition to strengthening close cooperation between the federal government and the Johor government,” he said in a statement issued today.

items.title: BCorp fully exits Citaglobal, nets RM42.56mil stake sale
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Corp Bhd (BCorp) has sold all its 8.64 per cent stake in Citaglobal Bhd to Detik Ria Sdn Bhd, a company linked to Tunku Tun Aminah Sultan Ibrahim, for RM42.56 million. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, BCorp said its wholly-owned Bernama Securities Sdn Bhd sold 46.77 million Citaglobal shares via a direct business transaction on June 19. Detik Ria is a major shareholder of BCorp.

items.title: Groups submit draft law seeking 30pc women candidates by GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A coalition of civil society organisations today submitted a memorandum and draft legislation to the government, calling for political parties to field at least 30 per cent women candidates by the next general election. The coalition said legislative intervention was needed to address the persistent under-representation of women in Malaysian politics. Former Petaling Jaya MP Maria Chin Abdullah said a legal framework was necessary to ensure women's participation was no longer dependent on political goodwill.

items.title: Police finalising investigation paper on man defaming Tok Batin hiding Jaslinda
items.summary: IPOH, June 22 — Police are finalising the investigation papers regarding the defamation case on the Tok Batin of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu via a live broadcast on the Tik Tok platform regarding the discovery of the missing female climber, Jaslinda Saludin. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the investigation paper has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor for further action. “We have sent the investigation paper to the public prosecutor and we need to update the investigation.

items.title: Malaysia still far from 30pct women representation in politics and economy, says committee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Women's participation in politics and the economy in Malaysia remains relatively low, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Women, Children and Community Development member Zakri Hassan said this was despite Malaysian women achieving strong outcomes in education and health. He said data presented to the committee showed that women's representation in the Dewan Rakyat, Dewan Negara and state legislative assemblies remains well below the 30 per cent target.

items.title: Stepfather fined RM25,000 after pleading guilty to five molestation charges
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A retiree was fined RM25,000 by the magistrates' court here today after pleading guilty to five counts of molesting his stepdaughter at various locations, including inside a car, since August last year. The accused paid the fine. For the first to third charges, the man was accused of using criminal force to molest the 21-year-old victim at 5.30pm, 8pm and 11pm on Aug 30, Sept 10 and Sept 22 last year, respectively.

items.title: New JPJ branch will operate seven days a week says Transport Minister (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysians will soon be able to settle Road Transport Department (JPJ) matters seven days a week and until late at night at a new branch in Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS), in what Transport Minister Anthony Loke described as a new model for public service delivery. Officially opening the new JPJ Bandar Tasik Selatan branch today, Loke said the facility would operate from 8.30am to 10pm daily, becoming the first JPJ branch in Kuala Lumpur to offer services every day of the week. Located on the third floor of TBS, the branch replaces JPJ’s Bandar Sri Permaisuri office, which had been operating since 2006.

items.title: Bersama to contest 15 seats in Johor election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) said today it will contest 15 seats in the Johor state election next month, including seats currently held by Umno, DAP, PKR and Muda. In a joint statement, party leaders Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said the seats were chosen based on voter trends, six months of survey data, local party strength, candidate suitability and campaign capacity. The 15 seats are Bukit Naning, Mahkota, Tiram, Puteri Wangsa, Johor Jaya, Permas, Larkin, Stulang, Perling, Kempas, Skudai, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Permai, Bukit Batu and Senai.

items.title: Finally open for business: Transport minister confirms LRT3 line starts June 29, PM to officiate launch on Sunday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The long-awaited Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) line linking Shah Alam and surrounding areas is set to begin operations next Monday June 29, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced today. The line is expected to improve connectivity across the western corridor of the Klang Valley, serving key population centres including Petaling Jaya, Shah Alam and Klang while easing road congestion in one of the country’s busiest urban regions. The LRT3 line is expected to serve thousands of commuters daily and provide interchanges with existing rail networks, including the Kelana Jaya Line, Kajang Line and KTM services, further strengthening public transport integration across the Klang Valley.

items.title: Operators complain of high fees, Selangor studies ‘edu‑entertainment’ category for children’s playgrounds
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 22 — The Selangor state government is studying a proposal to reclassify indoor children’s playgrounds as ‘edu-entertainment’ to allow for a reduction in the entertainment duty imposed on such premises. State Tourism and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said the study was initiated following complaints from operators who are required to pay entertainment duty of up to RM6,390 annually, even though the activities offered include educational elements. For this purpose, Ng said Selangor will hold engagement sessions with Penang, as the state has successfully introduced the ‘edu-entertainment’ classification without amending its enactment.

items.title: Selangor rolls out RM30m voucher scheme, 50,000 families to get RM100 monthly aid
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 22 — A total of 50,000 families in Selangor are eligible to apply for assistance through the ‘Kita Selangor Voucher’ initiative starting from noon tomorrow. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the assistance, amounting to RM100 per month for six months, involves a total allocation of RM30 million, with applications to be submitted entirely online through the official portal at www.baucarkitaselangor.com. He said the target group was identified based on data on applicants who have not received any existing assistance, such as the Bantuan Kehidupan Sejahtera Selangor (BINGKAS) programme, and in accordance with the databases of government agencies, including the Social Welfare Department and the Selangor Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU).

items.title: Govt rolls out three-tier plan to cushion food producers from global cost pressures
items.summary: Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin said the government’s response is divided into short-, medium- and long-term measures aimed at reducing production costs and strengthening food supply resilience. Chan said the government was also increasing financial support for paddy farmers, including raising paddy planting incentives for the 2026 season from RM160 to RM300 per hectare. For the medium term, Chan said the government is focusing on improving productivity across existing food production systems, including Permanent Food Production Parks, livestock and aquaculture zones, and Integrated Agricultural Development Areas.

items.title: Lorry driver in FRU crash cleared of stolen cow case but still faces trial over deadly accident
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 22 — The lorry driver facing a charge of dangerous driving that caused the deaths of nine Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) members was acquitted and discharged by the Magistrate’s Court here today of a charge of assisting in concealing two stolen cows last year. Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against Rudi Zulkarnain Mat Radi, 46, at the end of the prosecution’s case. Deputy public prosecutor Nur Darwis Aghniya Halim Azizi appeared for the prosecution, while lawyers Mohd Hafizuddin Khan Norkhan and Nur Intan Syakieraah Zakaria represented the accused.

items.title: Two couples plead not guilty in Johor Bahru to charges of abusing and intimidating maids
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 22 — Two married couples claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court here today to charges of causing injury to a female domestic helper at a home here last year. The accused, Muhamad Hirzihaziq Abulaes, 30, and his wife Nurfarah Ainna Hazhar, 34, together with Muhamad Firdaus Sha’aban, and his wife Nurafiqah Nabilah Hazhar, both 33, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out before Magistrate Atifah Hazimah Wahab. In a separate Magistrate’s Court, Muhamad Firdaus Shaaban and Nurafiqah also faced two other charges of intentionally causing injury to two other maids, Yatina Yusnita and Siti Hajar, at a house in Jalan Delima 2/6, Taman Daya here between 2pm and 4pm on June 11.

items.title: Dennis Lim takes charge as Penang’s new police chief, vows peace, order and stronger community ties
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 22 — Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng, who was appointed new Penang police chief effective last Saturday, assumed his official duties at a handover ceremony today. He replaced Datuk Azizee Ismail at the handover ceremony at the Penang contingent police headquarters senior officers’ mess, which was witnessed by Bukit Aman Management Department director Datuk Seri Abdul Aziz Abdul Majid. As new Penang police chief, Lim stressed the commitment to ensure every police personnel carries out their duties professionally, with integrity and in compliance with the law and regulations.

items.title: Growing awareness sees Selangor children break silence, reporting abuse through Talian Kasih hotline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Growing awareness among children to report cases of abuse, bullying and neglect through the Talian Kasih 15999 hotline has contributed to the increase in reports received by the Selangor Social Welfare Department (JKM). Selangor JKM principal assistant director (Children Division) Roziah Osman said the rise in reports reflects the effectiveness of advocacy programmes in educating children about their rights and the channels available to seek help. In this regard, Roziah said Selangor JKM is targeting more than 500 tahfiz centres and religious boarding schools in the state through its Child Protection Advocacy programme, which will be expanded beginning July 27.

items.title: Nothing comes free: Study finds only 34pc of Malaysian content creators file income tax, many don’t think to declare gifts
items.summary: The report also said free products accounted for 46 per cent of a content creator’s overall income sources, higher than direct cash payments, which stood at 43 per cent. Only 34 per cent of the content creators polled have filed income tax and intend to continue doing so. Of the remaining 66 per cent, 14 per cent said they did not know that content creators must file income tax, while the majority said they were unsure if they qualified for income tax.

items.title: Malaysia has enough fertiliser stock for nine months, no immediate risk, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin today said Malaysia has about nine months’ worth of fertiliser stock and faces no immediate risk of disruption. He added that a proposal to establish a longer-term fertiliser buffer stock is still under review. “Based on engagement sessions with manufacturers and suppliers, Malaysia currently has sufficient fertiliser stock to last approximately nine months.

items.title: Coroner sets Oct 23 as final date for Sabah schoolgirl Zara Qairina inquest hearing
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 22 — The Coroner’s Court here has set Oct 23 as the final date for the inquest hearing into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir, after noting that the proceedings had already gone on for 74 days. Coroner Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan said the court must keep track of the progress of the inquest, taking into account that July would mark the first anniversary of Zara Qairina’s death, while September would mark one year since the inquest began. Counsel Shahlan Jufri, who represents Zara Qairina’s mother, informed the court that the understanding was that the proceedings today would continue with Zara Qairina’s step-sister, while the first expert witness this month would only be available tomorrow.

items.title: Govt tables Cybercrime Bill in Parliament to replace outdated computer crimes law, cover AI offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Cybercrime Bill 2026, which seeks to repeal the Computer Crimes Act 1997 (Act 563), was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. Ahmad Zahid said the Bill is intended, among other things, to repeal the Computer Crimes Act 1997 (Act 563) to ensure that Malaysia can meet its international obligations under the Budapest Convention (Council of Europe Convention on Cybercrime) and the United Nations Convention Against Cybercrime. “The Cybercrime Bill 2026 will provide regulatory and law enforcement powers relating to cybercrime and will be regulated by the National Cyber Security Agency (Nacsa) under the National Security Council (MKN), Prime Minister’s Department (JPM),” he said.

items.title: High Court reschedules ex-Youth and Sports official’s graft appeal to September amid defence application
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The appeal hearing of former Youth and Sports Ministry (KBS) finance division secretary Otman Arsahd against his 15-year jail sentence and RM284.2 million fine has been postponed to Sept 21. High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin allowed an application by the defence to vacate the hearing dates scheduled for June 22, 23 and 25. The court fixed Sept 21, 22 and 23 for the appeal hearing and ordered the appellant to file written submissions by Aug 28.

items.title: Gobind: Malaysia to tighten laws on AI-generated child sexual exploitation material, deepfakes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the government is moving to strengthen existing legislation to address artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled offences, including deepfakes, identity impersonation and AI-generated child sexual exploitation material. He said Malaysia already has legal safeguards in place for certain crimes, particularly those involving children, but stressed that the legal framework must evolve to keep pace with emerging AI-related risks. For example, if we look at children, we already have existing criminal laws.

items.title: Leadership change at Yayasan Peneraju as Ibrahim Sani to leave CEO post July 1
items.summary: Minister of Economy and YP Board of Trustees chairman Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said Ibrahim’s leadership and contributions in steering the agency during an important phase of the organisation’s transformation, particularly in strengthening its role as a more strategic and value-oriented Bumiputera talent development institution. Akmal Nasrullah said the leadership transition would not affect the continuity of YP’s operations and strategic agenda, including its target of supporting the development of 100,000 Bumiputera talents by 2030 and developing 10,000 individuals with active economic participation and ownership, in line with the aspirations of the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35). As part of this transformation, YP launched its Talent Bank in 2024 as a strategic platform for identifying and nurturing high-potential Bumiputera talents.

items.title: Govt may raise maximum compound for over 700 traffic offences to RM500
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The government is expected to seek an increase in the maximum compound rate for more than 700 road traffic offences to RM500 via proposed amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987, according to a report. The current maximum compound for various traffic summonses issued by the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the police, including reckless driving, speeding, driving without a valid licence or road tax, and running a red light, is RM300. According to the report, the proposed increase would cover offences under several provisions of the Act, including those relating to motor vehicle licensing, vehicle licences, driving without a valid licence, third-party insurance, vehicle inspection, registration numbers, parking offences, failure to obey traffic directions and obstruction of enforcement officers.

items.title: Court in Ipoh sets July 1 for case involving woman accused of attacking Sultan Nazrin Shah
items.summary: IPOH, June 22 — The Magistrate’s Court here has set July 1 for case management involving a woman charged with using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state-level National Day celebration last year. Magistrate Norizan Ridzuan fixed the date to record the plea of Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 41. The woman was charged with using criminal force against Sultan Nazrin by attempting to attack the ruler in front of the Ipoh Town Hall on Jalan Panglima Bukit Gantang Wahab at 8.20am on Aug 31, 2025.

items.title: Malaysia and Bangladesh vow to push Asean for lasting Rohingya solution, engage Myanmar authorities directly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — Malaysia and Bangladesh will strengthen cooperation through Asean mechanisms to find a lasting solution to the longstanding Rohingya refugee issue, including engagement with Myanmar’s authorities, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said both countries share a strong commitment to resolving the plight of Rohingya refugees currently residing in Bangladesh and Malaysia. He said Bangladesh looked forward to closer regional cooperation on several issues.

items.title: PM Anwar stresses migrant welfare as Tarique seeks more jobs, both demand fairer recruitment process
items.summary: Speaking at a joint press conference with Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today, Anwar said while foreign workers remained critical to Malaysia’s economic growth, both countries must ensure their welfare and families are protected. We must protect the interests of these workers and their families,” said Anwar. Tarique said Bangladesh had requested Malaysia to consider recruiting more Bangladeshi workers and reopening its labour market as soon as possible.

items.title: Malaysia’s subsale home prices climb 4.8pc to RM545,059, Kuala Lumpur breaks RM1m mark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysia’s average subsale property price rose 4.8 per cent year-on-year to RM545,059 in the first quarter this year (Q1 2026), while the average price in Kuala Lumpur broke through the RM1 million barrier. IQI co-founder and group chief executive officer Kashif Ansari said the average price of a resale home in Malaysia climbed nearly five per cent over the past year, which reflects buyer confidence in the market. In Kuala Lumpur, the country’s largest urban resale market, buyers paid 15 per cent more on average for subsale homes in the first quarter of 2026, compared to a year earlier.

items.title: Azalina sets out seven reforms to separate powers and tighten Public Prosecutor accountability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — A parliamentary committee has unveiled seven concrete proposals to reshape how Malaysia appoints its Public Prosecutor, including a fixed seven year term and annual reporting to Parliament. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the recommendations were part of a review of the Constitution (Amendment) (No. Azalina, who also chairs the special select committee on constitutional reform, said the Public Prosecutor should instead be appointed by the Yang di Pertuan Agong on the advice of the Judicial and Legal Service Commission.

items.title: Three elephants, RM53m allegations and a graft probe: MACC looks into Dara, Amoi and Kelat transfer from Taiping Zoo to Japan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into allegations that payments linked to the transfer of Taiping Zoo elephants Dara, Amoi and Kelat to Japan did not reach the government, amid claims involving transactions worth about RM53 million. The commission said the investigation followed calls by wildlife advocacy group Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia on June 18 for authorities to scrutinise the transfer. According to MACC, the group alleged that several payments related to the relocation had not reached the government as intended and linked several individuals to transactions worth about RM53 million.

items.title: Government studying new Bar Course as possible replacement for CLP, Kulasegaran tells Dewan Rakyat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The government plans to replace the Certificate in Legal Practice (CLP) with a new two-stage qualification route aimed at strengthening practical training for law graduates, Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said in Parliament today. He explained that the proposed new Bar Course (NBC) would involve a three-month online conversion programme on the Malaysian legal system, followed by a six-month postgraduate certificate focusing on legal practice skills, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. Kulasegaran said the conversion course would be conducted online through a system to be developed by the Legal Profession Qualifying Board (LPQB).

items.title: Economy minister says inflation still moderate despite Hormuz disruption, but food and logistics costs under pressure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysia’s inflation remains moderate despite global trade disruptions linked to tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, although cost pressures are rising across key sectors, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. However, Akmal said cost pressures were rising across several key sectors. Within the agriculture and food sectors, Akmal said 63 per cent of Malaysia’s fertiliser requirements depend on imports.
